stories.title: Zelensky-Trump Meeting Planned During July 28 US Visit
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to meet US President Donald Trump during a visit to the US on July 28, according to diplomatic sources cited by Ukrainian public broadcaster Suspilne. The trip is expected to include Zelensky’s attendance at the funeral of late US Senator Lindsey Graham as well as a direct meeting with Trump, Suspilne reported. JOIN US ON TELEGRAM Follow our coverage of the war on the @Kyivpost_official . “Volodymyr Zelensky’s visit to the US is scheduled for Sunday, July 28. During the trip, the president will attend Senator Lindsey Graham’s funeral and meet with President Donald Trump,” Suspilne reported, citing diplomatic sources.

stories.title: Avatar: Seven Havens Officially Reveals Korra's Earthbender Successor With October 9 Premiere
stories.summary: Published Jul 23, 2026, 6:06 PM EDT Leo Reyna is an entertainment journalist and editor with extensive experience covering anime, manga, and gaming. Before joining CBR, he contributed to major entertainment outlets including Static Media and Ranker, where he developed a reputation for insightful industry coverage and a deep understanding of fan culture. At CBR, Leo reports on key developments within the anime industry—examining topics such as production challenges, studio practices, and creative communities—while also spotlighting the passion and artistry of fandoms worldwide.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
stories.summary:
Liverpool have made an initial approach to Monaco for winger Maghnes Akliouche.

Sky Sports News understands the club have checked on the potential availability of the 24-year-old France international.

  • Transfer Centre LIVE! | Liverpool news & transfers🔴

stories.title: Saudi prince found dead in £600-a-night London hotel room as cause of death unearthed
stories.summary: A Saudi prince who was found dead in a lavish Kensington hotel room was almost three times over the drink-drive limit and had "fatal levels" of the party drug GHP in his system, an inquest heard. Abdullah bin Fahad bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Jalawi al Saud was found dead in his £600-a-night Marriott hotel room in Kensington on the 25 November last year. A cleaner found the prince, 29, lying in the bathroom of his locked fifth-floor room fully clothed. Hotel security and ambulance were called but were unable to resuscitate him . He was sadly pronounced dead at the scene.

stories.title: Fuzhou's jasmine tea industrial heritage fuels local cultural tourism growth
stories.summary: Fuzhou's jasmine tea industrial heritage fuels local cultural tourism growth Workers make jasmine tea at a company of a jasmine tea industrial park in Fuzhou, southeast China's Fujian Province, on July 22, 2026. Fuzhou's traditional jasmine tea scenting craftsmanship, a national intangible cultural heritage, is driving the city's cultural tourism development. Leveraging its century-old jasmine tea industrial heritage, the city has restored historic tea plantations and scenting workshops while developing tea-themed industrial, educational and cultural attractions.

stories.title: How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer
stories.summary: Up to 100 meteors per hour can typically be seen during the Perseids shower. July 21, 2026, 4:43 PM It's the time of the summer when the Perseids meteor shower lights up the night sky. The Perseids occur every year from about July 17 to Aug. 23 and typically peak around mid-August, according to the American Meteor Society . This year, the Perseids are expected to peak between Aug. 12 and 13, according to AMS. The Perseids meteor shower is known for its frequency of meteors – capable of producing between 50 and 100 visible meteors per hour in a clear dark sky, according to NASA .
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Twitch Allows Parents To Restrict Teens From Livestreaming 07/22/2026
stories.summary: by , July 21, 2026 For the first time since its initial launch in 2011, Twitch is introducing parental control features, which include the ability for guardians to block their teen’s ability to broadcast livestreams on the platform. In addition to content classification labels and default safety settings applied to teen accounts, Twitch is attempting to allow parents and guardians to have more power over what their teens -- age 13-17 -- are able to do on the platform. Parents can now create an account and link it with their teen’s account, giving them access to account-management features and a weekly email update regarding their teen’s on-platform activity.

stories.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
stories.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

stories.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Chandra Shekhar Azad: If your blood does not rage, it is water that...
stories.summary: Chandra Shekhar Azad, a fearless revolutionary of India's independence movement, famously declared his name as "Azad" meaning free, and on February 27, 1931, he embraced martyrdom at Alfred Park in Allahabad rather than surrender to the British. His quote, "If your blood does not rage, it is water that flows in your veins," encapsulates the spirit of courage and commitment to service for the motherland.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Viking’s Leisurely Danube Cruise from Regensburg to Budapest
stories.summary: In Budapest, the Viking Tor longship docks steps from elegant Andrássy Avenue, and the Széchenyi Chain Bridge that connects the Buda and Pest sides of the city on our “Romantic Danube” river cruise © Geri Bain. From our chaise lounges on the sundeck, we watch an ever-changing landscape: vineyards terraced into steep hillsides, imposing mountaintop castles, small villages built around soaring churches, and long stretches of forest and grasslands. We are cruising through Austria’s Wachau Valley on the 190-passenger Viking Tor, one of Viking’s virtually identical “Longships” that ply the Upper Danube between Germany and Hungary.

stories.title: Madeleine Raleigh Iris Coffee Lab | New coffee shop replacing shuttered diner in Raleigh's Smoky Hollow
stories.summary: FOOD & DRINK Friday, July 24, 2026 1:48AM Iris Coffee Lab shared that Madeleine Raleigh, a coffee and bread bakery, will soon open in the old Tucker Street Diner location. RALEIGH, N. C. (WTVD) -- A new business will replace the Tucker Street Diner in Raleigh's popular Smoky Hollow area. On Thursday, Iris Coffee Lab shared on social media that Madeleine Raleigh, a coffee and bread bakery, will soon open in the location. Shop owners said they're hoping to open by the end of the year. The news comes just days after Tucker Street Diner closed after less than 18 months in operation. A note on the door of the shuttered retro-styled restaurant thanked customers for their support.

stories.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
stories.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

stories.title: Newcastle Jets To Take On Adamstown Rosebud FC
stories.summary: The Newcastle Jets A-League Men’s Team are set to take on NPL NNSW side Adamstown Rosebud FC in a pre-season match this Saturday Founded in 1889, Adamstown Rosebud FC is one of Newcastle’s oldest and most historic clubs, having produced 28 Australian internationals including 48-cap Socceroo and former Manchester United youth player Ray Baartz, as well as Col Curran, who also represented Manchester United at youth level, as well as playing for Australia at the 1974 Fifa World Cup in West Germany. Between 1984 and 1986, Adamstown Rosebud competed in the NSL as Newcastle Rosebud United, winning the 1984 NSL Cup.

stories.title: Kyle Gordon Is The Paul Simon Of Parody Songwriters
stories.summary: "Mr. Jambo" proves that Gordon's music is generational satire The song of the summer is a Paul Simon parody track. That’s not a joke. It’s not an exaggeration. It’s a statement of fact following the release of Kyle Gordon’s hit new single, “Mr. Jambo,” a jam that took Simon’s 1987 song “Under African Skies,” mixed it up with Toto’s seminal hit single “Africa” and then rolled it around in the wizened earth of the Serengeti. Gordon, a long-time friend of the Cracked live show series , is doing something with the parody music space that is approaching “Weird Al” Yankovic levels of parody excellence.

stories.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
stories.summary: “Concert stage atmosphere with vibrant stage lighting, artist holding a national flag on stage in front of a cheering crowd, realistic live performance concert photography style. ” During her massive concert in Bogotá, Spanish singer Rosalía delivered one of the most emotional moments of the night by sending a warm shout-out to a group of Costa Rican fans who traveled all the way to Colombia just to see her perform live. The gesture, which quickly went viral across social media, highlighted the artist’s close connection with her Latin American fanbase and the dedication of concertgoers who cross borders for her live shows. 1.

stories.title: Massimo Bottura Turned A Modena Farmhouse Into One of Italy’s Most Coveted Stays
stories.summary: At Casa Maria Luigia, owners Massimo Bottura and Lara Gilmore welcome guests into their world of food, art, music, and community.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on goods from around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, starting Friday. The move targets imports linked to forced labour claims and follows the expiration of a temporary 10% tax introduced earlier this year. The White House says the duties aim to address human rights abuses and unfair trade practices.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester later on Friday. The branch of Downing Street in the North West will form part of the prime minister's aim to shift power from Whitehall and Westminster to regions across the country. Ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh will also operate regularly from the new site, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join them by the end of next year. On Friday in Manchester Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK.

stories.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
stories.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

stories.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
stories.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
stories.summary: SPOILER ALERT: This story contains spoilers from the series premiere of “ Stuart Fails to Save the Universe ,” now streaming on HBO Max. HBO Max’s “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” opens in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena, an alternative world where zombie hands poke out of the ground, giant moths terrorize, random tears in reality serve as vacuum portals to unknown parts of the galaxy and survivors barter with their last remaining canned goods. About the only thing still operating is the comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom (played by Kevin Sussman ), where the gang on “The Big Bang Theory” would sometimes hang out.
entities: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe

stories.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Kasper Dolberg reintegrated into Ajax squad as Celtic target’s exit stalls
stories.summary: Kasper Dolberg has been reintegrated into Ajax’s squad after being cut from their early pre-season friendlies. The striker, whose interest from Celtic was well-documented last summer, had seemingly been in limbo awaiting a move to Midtjylland. The Danish club confirmed talks with the striker, but these appear to have stalled for now. After featuring in their friendly against Olympiacos on Saturday, Dolberg has now been named in their travelling squad in Thursday’s Conference League qualifier against Vojvodina. Should Celtic try for Kasper Dolberg again? How much would you pay for him?

stories.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
stories.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a large-scale effort to steal technology from top U.S. AI models, specifically claiming it distilled capabilities from Anthropic's Fable AI to develop its K3 model. This allegation follows the recent unveiling of K3, which has drawn global attention for its advanced capabilities. The U.S. government is investigating Moonshot's access to restricted Nvidia servers used in training its models, raising concerns over national security and technological competition.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
stories.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A federal health advisory panel has recommended easing restrictions on several controversial peptides, voting 8-6 to allow pharmacies to produce injectable forms of substances like BPC-157 and TB-500. This decision, while not binding, could lead to increased marketing of these peptides for wellness and cosmetic purposes, particularly as Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. supports the move. Critics warn that the safety and efficacy of these peptides remain unproven, raising concerns about potential health risks.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co.
stories.summary: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co. Lauterbach, Borschow & Co. announced that Alfredo Villordo has earned his Certified Public Accountant license.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR defeated Hull FC 28-20 in a heated derby match on Thursday, marking their ninth consecutive victory over their rivals. Despite Hull FC's early 12-0 lead, Hull KR rallied with key tries from Tom Amone and Joe Burgess, sealing the win with a late score from Jordan Dezaria. This loss extends Hull FC's struggles to six straight defeats, leaving them second from the bottom in the Super League standings.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The government ministers visited him in hospital as mass demonstrations continue demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan following a violent police crackdown on Monday’s parliament march. The youth-led CJP movement, which began as an online satire against exam irregularities, has grown into one of India’s largest dissent campaigns since May.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

Parents can now create an account and link it with their teen’s account, giving them access to account-management features and a weekly email update regarding their teen’s on-platform activity.

offbeat.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
offbeat.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

offbeat.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
offbeat.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

offbeat.title: Quote of the day by Chandra Shekhar Azad: If your blood does not rage, it is water that...
offbeat.summary: Chandra Shekhar Azad, a fearless revolutionary of India's independence movement, famously declared his name as "Azad" meaning free, and on February 27, 1931, he embraced martyrdom at Alfred Park in Allahabad rather than surrender to the British. His quote, "If your blood does not rage, it is water that flows in your veins," encapsulates the spirit of courage and commitment to service for the motherland.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: Viking’s Leisurely Danube Cruise from Regensburg to Budapest
offbeat.summary: In Budapest, the Viking Tor longship docks steps from elegant Andrássy Avenue, and the Széchenyi Chain Bridge that connects the Buda and Pest sides of the city on our “Romantic Danube” river cruise © Geri Bain. From our chaise lounges on the sundeck, we watch an ever-changing landscape: vineyards terraced into steep hillsides, imposing mountaintop castles, small villages built around soaring churches, and long stretches of forest and grasslands. We are cruising through Austria’s Wachau Valley on the 190-passenger Viking Tor, one of Viking’s virtually identical “Longships” that ply the Upper Danube between Germany and Hungary.

offbeat.title: Madeleine Raleigh Iris Coffee Lab | New coffee shop replacing shuttered diner in Raleigh's Smoky Hollow
offbeat.summary: FOOD & DRINK Friday, July 24, 2026 1:48AM Iris Coffee Lab shared that Madeleine Raleigh, a coffee and bread bakery, will soon open in the old Tucker Street Diner location. RALEIGH, N. C. (WTVD) -- A new business will replace the Tucker Street Diner in Raleigh's popular Smoky Hollow area. On Thursday, Iris Coffee Lab shared on social media that Madeleine Raleigh, a coffee and bread bakery, will soon open in the location. Shop owners said they're hoping to open by the end of the year. The news comes just days after Tucker Street Diner closed after less than 18 months in operation. A note on the door of the shuttered retro-styled restaurant thanked customers for their support.

offbeat.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
offbeat.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

offbeat.title: Newcastle Jets To Take On Adamstown Rosebud FC
offbeat.summary: The Newcastle Jets A-League Men’s Team are set to take on NPL NNSW side Adamstown Rosebud FC in a pre-season match this Saturday Founded in 1889, Adamstown Rosebud FC is one of Newcastle’s oldest and most historic clubs, having produced 28 Australian internationals including 48-cap Socceroo and former Manchester United youth player Ray Baartz, as well as Col Curran, who also represented Manchester United at youth level, as well as playing for Australia at the 1974 Fifa World Cup in West Germany. Between 1984 and 1986, Adamstown Rosebud competed in the NSL as Newcastle Rosebud United, winning the 1984 NSL Cup.

offbeat.title: Kyle Gordon Is The Paul Simon Of Parody Songwriters
offbeat.summary: "Mr. Jambo" proves that Gordon's music is generational satire The song of the summer is a Paul Simon parody track. That’s not a joke. It’s not an exaggeration. It’s a statement of fact following the release of Kyle Gordon’s hit new single, “Mr. Jambo,” a jam that took Simon’s 1987 song “Under African Skies,” mixed it up with Toto’s seminal hit single “Africa” and then rolled it around in the wizened earth of the Serengeti. Gordon, a long-time friend of the Cracked live show series , is doing something with the parody music space that is approaching “Weird Al” Yankovic levels of parody excellence.

offbeat.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
offbeat.summary: “Concert stage atmosphere with vibrant stage lighting, artist holding a national flag on stage in front of a cheering crowd, realistic live performance concert photography style. ” During her massive concert in Bogotá, Spanish singer Rosalía delivered one of the most emotional moments of the night by sending a warm shout-out to a group of Costa Rican fans who traveled all the way to Colombia just to see her perform live. The gesture, which quickly went viral across social media, highlighted the artist’s close connection with her Latin American fanbase and the dedication of concertgoers who cross borders for her live shows. 1.

offbeat.title: Massimo Bottura Turned A Modena Farmhouse Into One of Italy’s Most Coveted Stays
offbeat.summary: At Casa Maria Luigia, owners Massimo Bottura and Lara Gilmore welcome guests into their world of food, art, music, and community.

offbeat.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
offbeat.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

offbeat.title: Kasper Dolberg reintegrated into Ajax squad as Celtic target’s exit stalls
offbeat.summary: Kasper Dolberg has been reintegrated into Ajax’s squad after being cut from their early pre-season friendlies. The striker, whose interest from Celtic was well-documented last summer, had seemingly been in limbo awaiting a move to Midtjylland. The Danish club confirmed talks with the striker, but these appear to have stalled for now. After featuring in their friendly against Olympiacos on Saturday, Dolberg has now been named in their travelling squad in Thursday’s Conference League qualifier against Vojvodina. Should Celtic try for Kasper Dolberg again? How much would you pay for him?

offbeat.title: Alan Hand becomes new President of Tullamore Lions Club
offbeat.summary: Dymphna Bracken and Alan Hand. Published: Thu 23 Jul 2026, 4:18 PM Alan Hand has been elected as the 52nd President of Tullamore Lions Club, succeeding outgoing president Dymphna Bracken. The official handover took place during the club’s annual changeover meeting at Giltraps of Kinnitty on Tuesday evening, where members gathered to mark the occasion and celebrate the club’s achievements over the past year. Alan previously served as the vice-president during the 25/26 term. Paying tribute to the outgoing president, the club thanked Dymphna for her commitment during her term. “We would like to thank Dymphna for her trojan work in the last 12 months,” the club said in a statement.

offbeat.title: Italian Police Union Defends Officers After Killing of Abderrahim Fakir
offbeat.summary: Rabat- The Italian Police Workers' Union (Sindacato Italiano Unitario Lavoratori Polizia, SIULP) has defended the officers involved in the police intervention that resulted in the killing of Moroccan migrant Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna, even as the case continues to fuel public outrage, protests, and calls for accountability in Italy and Morocco.

offbeat.title: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co.
offbeat.summary: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co. Lauterbach, Borschow & Co. announced that Alfredo Villordo has earned his Certified Public Accountant license.

offbeat.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
offbeat.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The government ministers visited him in hospital as mass demonstrations continue demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan following a violent police crackdown on Monday’s parliament march. The youth-led CJP movement, which began as an online satire against exam irregularities, has grown into one of India’s largest dissent campaigns since May.

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
breaking.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on goods from around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, starting Friday. The move targets imports linked to forced labour claims and follows the expiration of a temporary 10% tax introduced earlier this year. The White House says the duties aim to address human rights abuses and unfair trade practices.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester later on Friday. The branch of Downing Street in the North West will form part of the prime minister's aim to shift power from Whitehall and Westminster to regions across the country. Ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh will also operate regularly from the new site, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join them by the end of next year. On Friday in Manchester Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

politics.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

facts.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
facts.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

facts.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
facts.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

facts.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
facts.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

entertainment.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
entertainment.summary: SPOILER ALERT: This story contains spoilers from the series premiere of “ Stuart Fails to Save the Universe ,” now streaming on HBO Max. HBO Max’s “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” opens in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena, an alternative world where zombie hands poke out of the ground, giant moths terrorize, random tears in reality serve as vacuum portals to unknown parts of the galaxy and survivors barter with their last remaining canned goods. About the only thing still operating is the comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom (played by Kevin Sussman ), where the gang on “The Big Bang Theory” would sometimes hang out.
entities: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe

crisis.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a large-scale effort to steal technology from top U.S. AI models, specifically claiming it distilled capabilities from Anthropic's Fable AI to develop its K3 model. This allegation follows the recent unveiling of K3, which has drawn global attention for its advanced capabilities. The U.S. government is investigating Moonshot's access to restricted Nvidia servers used in training its models, raising concerns over national security and technological competition.

crisis.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Hull KR defeated Hull FC 28-20 in a heated derby match on Thursday, marking their ninth consecutive victory over their rivals. Despite Hull FC's early 12-0 lead, Hull KR rallied with key tries from Tom Amone and Joe Burgess, sealing the win with a late score from Jordan Dezaria. This loss extends Hull FC's struggles to six straight defeats, leaving them second from the bottom in the Super League standings.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A federal health advisory panel has recommended easing restrictions on several controversial peptides, voting 8-6 to allow pharmacies to produce injectable forms of substances like BPC-157 and TB-500. This decision, while not binding, could lead to increased marketing of these peptides for wellness and cosmetic purposes, particularly as Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. supports the move. Critics warn that the safety and efficacy of these peptides remain unproven, raising concerns about potential health risks.

health.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
health.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.
